text.title: Laravel Patterns Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed in real Laravel codebases.

antipattern.symptom: OrderController grows to 180+ lines; controller methods open transactions, hit Eloquent, format responses inline.
antipattern.root-cause: No service layer; all logic shoved into controller.
antipattern.fix: Extract to OrderService; controller method becomes 5-10 lines calling service + returning Resource.

antipattern.symptom: API exposes timestamps + soft-delete flag + foreign keys to consumers; client breaks on schema migration.
antipattern.fix: Wrap in OrderResource; explicitly list fields; version the resource (V1, V2).

antipattern.symptom: Validation rules duplicated across Store + Update; messages inconsistent; auth check intermixed.
antipattern.root-cause: `$request->validate([...])` inline in controller instead of Form Request.
antipattern.fix: Generate StoreOrderRequest + UpdateOrderRequest; both extend OrderRequest with shared rules in protected method.

antipattern.symptom: Authorization scattered across controller `if ($user->id !== $order->user_id) abort(403)` checks.
antipattern.root-cause: No Policy class; logic duplicated and untestable.
antipattern.fix: Generate OrderPolicy; register in AuthServiceProvider; call `$this->authorize('update', $order)` from controller or `$this->user()->can(...)` from Form Request.
